questions.text: DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA Answer ALL TWENTY FIVE questions. Write your answers in the spaces provided. You must write down all the stages in your working. 1 Without using a calculator, work out 3 2 3 2 4 7 × Show each stage of your working. Give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form. ......................................................... (Total for Question 1 is 3 marks)

*P81639A0328* Turn over 3   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 2 The height of a tree is 17.8 metres correct to one decimal place. (a) Write down the lower bound for the height of the tree. ........................................................... metres (1) The weight of an apple is 145 grams correct to the nearest 5 grams. (b) Write down the upper bound for the weight of the apple. ........................................................... grams (1) A 4 766 815 0 399 . . (c) By rounding each number to one significant figure, work out an estimate for the value of A Show your working clearly. .......................................................... (2) (Total for Question 2 is 4 marks)

*P81639A0428* 4   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 3 Here is a biased spinner. The spinner can land on pink or on green or on red or on yellow or on blue. pink green red yellow blue The table gives information about the probability that, when the spinner is spun, it will land on each colour. Colour pink green red yellow blue Probability 0.14 0.23 2x 0.18 7x Jim is going to spin the spinner 500 times. Work out the expected number of times the spinner will land on blue. .......................................................... (Total for Question 3 is 4 marks)

*P81639A0528* Turn over 5   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 4 The diagram shows triangle ABC and a semicircle. Diagram NOT accurately drawn A B C 20 cm 8 cm 15 cm 25 cm AB = 15 cm BC = 20 cm AC = 25 cm angle ABC 90 The diameter of the semicircle is 8 cm Work out the area of the region shown shaded in the diagram. Give your answer correct to 3 significant figures. ......................................................... cm2 (Total for Question 4 is 3 marks)

*P81639A0728* Turn over 7   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA D DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 6 The diagram shows a block in the shape of a cuboid. Diagram NOT accurately drawn x m 6 m The block has a horizontal base with length 6 m and width x m The block is placed on a table so that the whole of its horizontal base is in contact with the table. The block exerts a force of 702 newtons on the table. The pressure on the table due to the block is 65 newtons/m² Work out the value of x x = ....................................................... (Total for Question 6 is 3 marks) force pressure = area

*P81639A01128* Turn over 11   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 10 A B C D 12 cm 47 cm 60° Diagram NOT accurately drawn ABCD is a trapezium with one line of symmetry. angle ADC = 60° AD = 12 cm DC = 47 cm Work out the area of the trapezium. Give your answer correct to 3 significant figures. Show your working clearly. ....................................................... cm2 (Total for Question 10 is 5 marks)

*P81639A01228* 12   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 11 (a) Expand and simplify 5 5 4 2 3 x x x ....................................................... (3) (b) Express 9 4 7 5 y y as a single fraction in its simplest form. ....................................................... (3) (Total for Question 11 is 6 marks)

*P81639A01328* Turn over 13   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN T 12 Ricardo is going to play one game of badminton and one game of tennis. He can either win or lose each game. The probability that he will win the game of badminton is 0.7 The probability that he will win the game of tennis is 0.4 (a) Complete the probability tree diagram. lose win badminton tennis lose win lose win ................ ................ ................ ................ ................ ................ (2) (b) Work out the probability that Ricardo loses both games. ....................................................... (2) (Total for Question 12 is 4 marks)

*P81639A01428* THIS AREA 14   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 13 (a) Complete the table of values for y = x3 + 2x + 3 x –3 –2 –1 0 1 2 3 y –9 0 3 6 36 (1) (b) On the grid, draw the graph of y = x3 + 2x + 3 for –3  x  3 –10 –20 –30 –2 –3 –1 2 1 3 10 O 20 30 40 y x (2) (Total for Question 13 is 3 marks)

*P81639A01528* Turn over 15   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 14 The diagram shows sector OAB of a circle, centre O Diagram NOT accurately drawn 40° A B O 12 cm OA OB = = 12 cm angle AOB 40 (a) Work out the area of the sector. *P81639A01728* Turn over 17   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AR 17 3 3 5 k where k is an integer. Show each stage of your working clearly. (3) (Total for Question 17 is 4 marks)

*P81639A02228* 22   DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA DO NOT WRITE IN THIS AREA 22 A box contains 20 counters. 9 of the counters are red 7 of the counters are yellow 4 of the counters are green Alex takes at random three counters from the box. Work out the probability that exactly two of the three counters are the same colour. ....................................................... (Total for Question 22 is 3 marks)

P is the point with coordinates (4, 7) R is the point with coordinates (8, –5) Find an equation of the straight line that passes through the points Q and S Give your answer in the form ay = bx + c where a, b and c are integers. .......................................................
